ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-22-2012 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE; ADDING CASE TO BRAYTON GROUP 600; AND TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS SEVERED FROM BRAYTON GROUP 595 AND ADDED TO GROUP 600. THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED TO MAY 7, 2012 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT FURTHER GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S CCP 437C(H) REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE OF DEFENDANT DAL-TILE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DISCOVERY IS REOPENED SOLELY AS TO DEFENDANT DAL-TILE ONLY. PLAINTIFF IS ALLOWED TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY RELATING TO INFORMATION DISCLOSED IN JOHN TURNER'S DEPOSITION REGARDING DAL-TILE'S ALLEGED RESPONSIBILITY. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 27, 2012 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. A FURTHER ORDER CONTINUING THE HEARING SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAL-TILE CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Edward E. Hartley of Hassard Bonnington LLP (288.9800) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections based on the execution of Mr. Turner's document are overruled, however his statements are insufficient to sustain defendant's initial burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar as they do not negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Mr. Slade's deposition testimony and declaration and defendant's March 2011 and January 1998 responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Mr. Ay's declaration is admissible regarding dry products and that when it is mixed generates dust at various levels. Defendant's objections to the American Olean catalogs are sustained. Defendant's objections to Jeffrey Schott's deposition testimony are sustained. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of Aiken Welch, CSR #6099 (503/TLJ)
